__TR_saas-buyer-risk-assessment_48__: Here are three real signals Churn Lens flagged in recent uploads:
__TR_saas-buyer-risk-assessment_49__: The "Stable MRR" that was actually declining:
__TR_saas-buyer-risk-assessment_4__: SaaS Buyer-Side Risk Assessment — Free Tool
__TR_saas-buyer-risk-assessment_50__: Target showed $40K MRR flat over 12 months. Churn Lens computed the churn-adjusted projection: $31K MRR by month 18 without new sales. The flat MRR was entirely growth-masked churn (Trick #7).
__TR_saas-buyer-risk-assessment_52__: HHI came back at 3,800. The top 3 customers (a law firm, a university, and a government agency) accounted for 51% of MRR. Two had expiring contracts within 6 months. The deal was re-priced with a 25% concentration discount.
__TR_saas-buyer-risk-assessment_54__: Target reported 90% NRR. Churn Lens found expansion revenue of 15% (from upsells) and contraction of 40% (from downgrades) — the net was 90% but the contraction was a leading indicator of value erosion that wouldn't show up in the headline number.
